message:
refactor(tmux): DRY pane parsing, secure FIFO directory, and robust content hashing

Pane parsing consolidation:
- Extract parsePaneLine() and parsePaneFromParts() to replace three
  identical copies of the pane-parsing loop body in GetPanesContext(),
  GetAllPanesContext(), and GetPanesWithActivityContext(). Each had
  ~40 lines of strconv.Atoi, field assignment, parseAgentFromTitle,
  and detectAgentFromCommand -- now a single 30-line function. This
  eliminates a maintenance hazard where a bug fix in one parser would
  need to be replicated in the other two.

Remote buffer loading fix:
- loadBufferRemote() now pipes content through ssh stdin instead of
  embedding it in a shell-quoted command-line argument. The previous
  approach could hit ARG_MAX limits on large prompts (Linux default
  128KB) causing silent failures when injecting multi-page work items
  into remote agent sessions.

FIFO directory security:
- Replace hardcoded /tmp/ntm_pane_streams with a user-specific
  directory under XDG_STATE_HOME (~/.local/state/ntm/streams by
  default). The shared /tmp location was vulnerable to symlink attacks
  where another user could pre-create the FIFO path and intercept
  agent output streams.

Content deduplication:
- Replace simpleHash() (which used length + first/last 32 chars) with
  FNV-64a hash. The old approach produced collisions when content
  changed only in the middle -- common for agent output where headers
  and footers remain constant while the working area changes. Tests
  updated to expect the new fixed-width 16-char hex output.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
